wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the RDMM project overview section focused on the unified developer desktop toolkit. Explain its purpose as a Tauri-based desktop application that integrates multiple development tools into a single interface. Document the core architecture combining React frontend with Rust backend, the plugin-based extensible design, and the target audience of developers and DevOps professionals. Include the key value propositions, supported platforms (Windows, macOS, Linux), and the technology stack overview. Provide practical examples of common workflows and use cases. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including terms like 'plugin registry', 'Tauri commands', and 'state management'.

wiki_catalogs.name: MongoDB Client
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the MongoDB client plugin focused on NoSQL database management within RDMM. Explain the connection management system, authentication mechanisms, and connection pooling strategies. Document the database browser interface for exploring collections and documents, the query workspace for executing MongoDB queries, and the document browser for viewing and editing individual documents. Detail the server status monitoring capabilities, import/export functionality, and index management tools. Include practical examples of connecting to MongoDB instances, executing CRUD operations, querying with aggregation pipelines, and managing database schemas. Address performance optimization techniques, connection security, error handling, and best practices for MongoDB administration. Explain the integration with RDMM's plugin architecture and shared state management patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Shell
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's application shell system focused on the main layout container and desktop window management. Explain the AppShell component architecture including the Ant Design Layout structure, sidebar integration, and plugin routing system. Document the title bar implementation with platform-specific behaviors for Windows and macOS, including native title bar support and window controls. Detail the status bar functionality with real-time system information display, LAN chat integration, and runtime detection. Include practical examples of customizing the layout, managing window resizing with edge overlays, and implementing cross-platform desktop features. Address responsive design patterns, accessibility considerations, and integration with the plugin architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Plugin Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for RDMM's plugin architecture system. Explain the plugin registry pattern, manifest-based plugin registration, and dynamic loading mechanisms. Document the plugin lifecycle from registration to execution, including visibility controls and plugin metadata management. Detail the plugin interface contracts, state management integration, and communication patterns with the host application. Include practical examples of plugin development, manifest creation, and integration with the plugin router. Address plugin isolation, security considerations, and performance optimization strategies. Provide guidelines for extending the system with custom plugins and maintaining backward compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: SSH Client
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's SSH client plugin focused on secure remote terminal access and connection management. Explain the integrated SSH connection management system, terminal emulation capabilities, key-based authentication, and port tunneling features. Document the connection form configuration, session management, terminal tab interface, and SSH key storage. Detail the implementation of SSH protocols, encryption methods, and security best practices. Include practical examples of establishing SSH connections, configuring tunnel rules, managing SSH keys, and using the terminal workspace. Address common SSH authentication issues, connection troubleshooting, and performance optimization for remote terminal sessions.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Debugger
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the API Debugger plugin focused on HTTP request testing and debugging. Explain the four-tab interface including Workspace for request composition, Collections for organizing endpoints, Environments for variable management, and History for tracking previous requests. Document the request/response handling workflow, authentication methods, header management, and body formatting. Detail collection organization, environment variable substitution, and history tracking mechanisms. Include practical examples of API testing scenarios, debugging techniques, and workflow optimization. Address integration with external APIs, response validation, and performance testing patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tauri Commands
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's Tauri command system focused on frontend-backend communication architecture. Explain the command registration process, parameter serialization/deserialization, and async operation handling. Document the security model including capability-based permissions, window management, and IPC protocols. Detail the command lifecycle from frontend invocation to backend execution, including error propagation and response handling. Include practical examples of implementing custom commands, handling complex data types, and managing long-running operations. Address debugging techniques, performance optimization, and best practices for command design. Document the relationship between Tauri commands and the plugin system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Connection Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for MongoDB connection management focused on establishing and maintaining database connections within RDMM. Document the connection form interface for configuring MongoDB connection strings, authentication methods (username/password, SCRAM-SHA-1, SCRAM-SHA-256, X.509 certificates), SSL/TLS settings, and connection pooling parameters. Explain the connection list view for managing multiple connections, connection validation processes, and automatic reconnection logic. Detail the connection state management, error handling for connection failures, timeout configurations, and security considerations including credential encryption. Include practical examples of connecting to local MongoDB instances, cloud MongoDB clusters (Atlas), replica sets, and sharded clusters. Address connection lifecycle management, resource cleanup, and best practices for secure connection handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Connection Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the MySQL connection management system. Document the connection list interface for viewing, adding, editing, and removing MySQL connections. Explain the connection form component with host configuration, authentication methods (username/password, SSH tunneling), SSL settings, and connection pooling parameters. Detail the connection store architecture using Zustand for state management, including active connection tracking, connection validation, and error handling. Cover connection security features like encrypted credential storage, SSH tunnel integration, and connection testing. Include practical examples of establishing secure connections, configuring connection pools, handling connection failures, and implementing connection lifecycle management. Address best practices for connection management, performance optimization, and troubleshooting common connection issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Connection Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the S3 connection management system. Document the connection store architecture for managing multiple S3 endpoints, credential handling for AWS access keys and secret keys, and secure connection establishment. Explain the connection form interface for configuring endpoint URLs, region settings, and authentication parameters. Detail connection validation, testing procedures, and error handling for connectivity issues. Include practical examples of adding new connections, switching between active connections, managing connection credentials securely, and handling connection failures. Address security best practices for credential storage, connection pooling, and authentication token management. Document the integration with RDMM's encryption layer and connection lifecycle management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Connection Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Redis connection management system. Document the connection list interface for viewing and managing multiple Redis instances, connection form configuration for establishing new connections with host, port, password, and authentication settings. Explain connection pooling strategies, connection validation, error handling, and connection lifecycle management. Include practical examples of adding connections, configuring authentication methods (password, ACL), SSL/TLS settings, and connection testing procedures. Address connection security best practices, timeout configurations, and connection recovery mechanisms. Document the connection store architecture, state management patterns, and integration with the plugin registry system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Titlebar Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the titlebar management system focused on cross-platform window controls and native title bar integration. Explain the platform-specific implementation differences between Windows and macOS, including native title bar support detection and Tauri window control integration. Document the titlebar component architecture, window button handling (minimize, maximize, close), and drag region functionality. Include practical examples of customizing titlebar appearance, handling window state changes, and implementing platform-aware behaviors. Address Tauri-specific window management APIs, event handling patterns, and integration with the broader application shell. Cover accessibility considerations, keyboard shortcuts, and responsive titlebar layouts.

wiki_catalogs.name: SSH Connection Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for SSH connection management functionality. Document the connection list interface, connection form configuration, and connection lifecycle management. Explain connection creation, editing, deletion, and validation processes. Detail supported authentication methods including password-based and key-based authentication. Cover connection pooling, reconnection logic, and connection state management. Include practical examples of creating SSH connections, configuring connection parameters, and troubleshooting connection failures. Address connection security best practices, credential storage, and connection performance optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Settings Store
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the settings store implementation in RDMM's state management system. Explain the SettingsState interface and its properties including sidebarCollapsed, dbToolsCollapsed, and selectedPluginId. Document the Zustand store creation pattern with persist middleware for local storage integration. Detail the setter functions and their usage patterns throughout the application. Include practical examples of accessing and modifying settings state, implementing reactive UI updates, and integrating with the plugin system. Address state persistence mechanisms, default values, and migration strategies for settings changes across versions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Request Workspace
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Request Workspace component focused on composing and executing HTTP requests. Document the request composition interface including URL input, method selection (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.), header management, and body formatting options. Explain authentication methods (Bearer tokens, API keys, OAuth), query parameter handling, and form data encoding. Detail response visualization including status codes, headers, body parsing, and timing information. Include practical examples of common API testing scenarios, debugging techniques for failed requests, and performance optimization strategies. Address request validation, error handling, and response caching mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Connection Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the MQ client connection management system. Explain the connection configuration for both Kafka and RabbitMQ brokers, including connection string formats, authentication methods, and security settings. Document the connection lifecycle from creation to destruction, including connection validation, reconnection logic, and error handling. Detail the connection store architecture, state management for active connections, and connection switching mechanisms. Include practical examples of configuring different message broker types, handling connection failures, and managing multiple concurrent connections. Address connection pooling strategies, timeout configurations, and best practices for secure connection management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Editor Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Confluence Editor interface component. Document the markdown editing functionality, real-time preview capabilities, and user interaction patterns. Explain the editor state management, document persistence, and collaborative editing features. Include practical examples of markdown syntax usage, formatting options, and content editing workflows. Address the integration with the Confluence API for document synchronization and conflict resolution. Document keyboard shortcuts, auto-save functionality, and document versioning within the editor interface.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for RDMM focused on quick onboarding and initial setup. Document system requirements, installation procedures for different platforms, and first-time configuration steps. Include step-by-step instructions for running the development environment, building the application, and launching the desktop app. Explain the basic navigation, plugin activation, and initial setup workflows. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common installation issues. Include practical examples of connecting your first database, configuring SSH connections, and using core features. Make content accessible to developers new to desktop application development while providing sufficient technical depth.

wiki_catalogs.name: MySQL Client
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the MySQL client plugin focused on relational database management within RDMM. Explain the connection management system, authentication mechanisms, and connection pooling strategies for MySQL instances. Document the database browser interface for exploring schemas, tables, and indexes, the SQL workspace for executing queries and DDL statements, and the table data viewer for browsing and editing records. Detail the server status monitoring, import/export tools for data migration, and schema management capabilities. Include practical examples of connecting to MySQL databases, executing SELECT queries, managing table structures, importing/exporting data, and optimizing database performance. Address connection security, transaction handling, error recovery, and best practices for MySQL administration. Explain the integration with RDMM's plugin architecture and state management patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Sidebar Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for RDMM's sidebar navigation system focused on plugin selection and menu management. Explain the sidebar component implementation including plugin listing, selection handling, and visibility controls. Document the plugin visibility system with conditional rendering based on user preferences and plugin capabilities. Detail the sidebar collapse/expand functionality, responsive behavior, and integration with the plugin registry. Include practical examples of customizing sidebar appearance, adding new navigation items, and implementing plugin-specific sidebar features. Address accessibility considerations, keyboard navigation, and mobile-responsive design patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for RDMM's state management architecture using Zustand. Explain the store pattern implementation, global state coordination, and plugin-specific state management. Document the state store structure, actions, selectors, and middleware integration. Detail the relationship between application-wide stores (settings, theme) and plugin-specific stores (connections, workspaces). Include practical examples of state subscriptions, state persistence, and cross-plugin state sharing. Address state hydration, error handling, and performance optimization techniques. Provide guidelines for creating new stores, managing side effects, and maintaining state consistency across the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: MQ Client
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's MQ client plugin focused on message queue management and monitoring for Kafka and RabbitMQ systems. Explain the connection management for different message brokers, message browsing interfaces, and real-time message inspection capabilities. Document the implementation of message studio for crafting and sending messages, history tracking for message operations, and protocol-specific connection handling. Detail the integration patterns for Kafka and RabbitMQ, message serialization formats, and consumer/producer operations. Include practical examples of connecting to message queues, browsing message topics/channels, sending test messages, and monitoring message flow. Address message queue troubleshooting, performance considerations, and best practices for message broker administration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Confluence Editor
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Confluence Editor plugin focused on collaborative documentation and publishing workflows. Explain the markdown editing interface, real-time preview functionality, and page tree navigation. Document the publishing workflow to Confluence, attachment management, and document conversion processes. Detail the sidebar navigation for page hierarchy, publish dialog configuration, and integration with Atlassian Confluence services. Include practical examples of collaborative document creation, publishing workflows, and content management strategies. Address markdown syntax support, image handling, and document versioning within the Confluence ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Repositories
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's database repository pattern focused on data access abstraction and connection management. Explain the repository interface design, connection pooling strategies, and transaction handling. Document the implementation patterns for different database types including MongoDB, MySQL, Redis, S3, and SSH connections. Detail the connection lifecycle, error handling, and retry mechanisms. Include practical examples of implementing new repository types, managing connection state, and optimizing query performance. Address security considerations including credential storage, encryption, and access control. Document the relationship between repositories and the plugin command system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Browser
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the MongoDB database browser interface focused on exploring and navigating database collections within RDMM. Document the hierarchical tree view for browsing databases and collections, collection filtering and search capabilities, and metadata display including document counts, storage sizes, and collection statistics. Explain the context menu operations for creating, dropping, and managing collections, as well as database-level operations like creating new databases and dropping existing ones. Detail the integration with the active connection management, real-time refresh mechanisms, and performance optimization for large datasets. Include practical examples of browsing collection structures, understanding collection properties, and navigating complex database schemas. Address user experience considerations for handling large numbers of collections and efficient data loading strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Browser
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the MySQL database browser interface. Document the schema exploration functionality for navigating databases, tables, views, and stored procedures. Explain the hierarchical tree view showing database objects with expandable nodes for schemas, tables, indexes, and foreign keys. Detail the index manager component for creating, modifying, and deleting database indexes with visual representations and performance impact analysis. Cover object metadata display including table statistics, column definitions, constraint information, and query execution plans. Include practical examples of browsing database schemas, analyzing table structures, managing indexes for performance optimization, and understanding database relationships. Address filtering capabilities, search functionality, and integration with other MySQL client features for seamless database exploration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Bucket Browser
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the S3 bucket browsing functionality. Document the bucket list interface for exploring available storage buckets, bucket filtering and search capabilities, and bucket metadata display. Explain bucket settings configuration including versioning, lifecycle policies, and access control lists. Detail bucket operations such as creation, deletion, and permission management. Include practical examples of browsing bucket contents, applying filters, viewing bucket statistics, and configuring bucket properties. Address pagination handling for large bucket lists, performance optimization for bucket enumeration, and error handling for bucket access issues. Document the integration with AWS SDK operations and real-time bucket status updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Key Browser and Editors
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Redis key browsing and editing system. Detail the key tree navigation interface for exploring hierarchical key structures, key filtering and search capabilities, and real-time key metadata display including data types and TTL values. Document the individual key editors for different Redis data types: string editor for simple key-value pairs, list editor for ordered collections with pagination, set editor for unique value collections, hash editor for field-value maps, and sorted set editor for score-based ordered sets. Include practical examples of browsing keys, editing different data types, managing key expiration, bulk operations, and performance optimization techniques. Address key visualization patterns, memory usage insights, and data export capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Status Bar System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the status bar system focused on real-time system information display and application status monitoring. Explain the status bar architecture including dynamic status item generation, runtime detection, and LAN chat integration. Document the status items structure with label-value pairs, badge counting systems, and conditional rendering logic. Include practical examples of adding custom status items, implementing real-time updates, and managing status bar visibility. Address the integration with LAN chat unread message tracking, device connectivity monitoring, and application runtime detection. Cover styling patterns, responsive behavior, and accessibility considerations for status bar content.

wiki_catalogs.name: Theme Store
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the theme store implementation in RDMM's state management system. Explain the theme state management including dark/light mode switching, color scheme coordination, and styling integration. Document the theme provider setup, CSS variable management, and component styling patterns. Detail the reactive theme updates and their impact on the overall application appearance. Include practical examples of theme customization, implementing custom themes, and coordinating theme changes across different UI components. Address performance considerations for theme switching and accessibility compliance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Terminal Emulation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the SSH terminal emulation system. Detail the terminal workspace interface, terminal tab management, and session handling. Explain terminal rendering, command execution, and interactive shell support. Document terminal customization options, keyboard shortcuts, and command history. Cover terminal session persistence, tab switching, and multi-session management. Include practical examples of terminal usage, command execution, and terminal configuration. Address terminal performance optimization, session recovery, and troubleshooting common terminal issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Collections View
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Collections View component focused on organizing and managing API endpoints. Document collection creation, folder structure organization, and endpoint grouping strategies. Explain import/export functionality for sharing collections, template creation for reusable request patterns, and batch operation capabilities. Detail collection sharing mechanisms, version control integration, and collaborative workflow features. Include practical examples of organizing large API suites, creating development workflows, and maintaining collection documentation. Address collection synchronization, conflict resolution, and backup strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Message Browser
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the MQ client message browser functionality. Explain the interface for browsing topics/channels in Kafka and exchanges/queues in RabbitMQ. Document message listing, filtering, and pagination capabilities. Detail the message inspection interface including metadata viewing, payload examination, and message property analysis. Include examples of navigating different broker architectures, searching for specific messages, and analyzing message flow patterns. Address message consumption patterns, offset management, and real-time message monitoring. Document the integration with different message formats and encoding schemes used in various broker implementations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Publishing Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Confluence publishing workflow. Detail the publish dialog interface, document conversion processes, and publishing configuration options. Explain the integration with Confluence API endpoints, authentication mechanisms, and document transformation pipelines. Document the publishing stages, error handling, and retry mechanisms. Include practical examples of publishing workflows, document metadata management, and version control integration. Address publishing permissions, content validation, and conflict resolution strategies during the publishing process.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for RDMM's system design and component organization. Describe the high-level architecture combining React frontend with Tauri/Rust backend, explaining the plugin-based extensible design pattern. Document the plugin registry system, state management architecture using Zustand, and the frontend-backend communication via Tauri commands. Explain the separation of concerns between UI components, plugin implementations, and backend services. Include system boundaries, data flow patterns, and integration points. Address the technology stack decisions, cross-cutting concerns like security and encryption, and deployment considerations. Provide architectural diagrams showing component relationships and data flow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Redis Manager
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Redis manager plugin focused on in-memory data structure server management within RDMM. Explain the connection management system for Redis instances, authentication mechanisms, and connection pooling strategies. Document the key browser interface for exploring Redis keys, data types, and TTL values, the console for executing Redis commands, and the server information panel for monitoring instance statistics. Detail the key editors for different data types (strings, lists, sets, hashes, sorted sets), TTL management, and memory optimization tools. Include practical examples of connecting to Redis instances, browsing key-value pairs, executing Redis commands, managing data expiration, and monitoring server performance. Address connection security, pipeline operations, error handling, and best practices for Redis administration. Explain the integration with RDMM's plugin architecture and real-time update mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's state management system focused on Zustand-based stores for application-wide state. Explain the settings store implementation including user preferences, plugin selections, and UI state management. Document the theme store with dark/light mode support, color schemes, and styling coordination. Detail the state persistence mechanisms, reactive updates, and integration with the plugin system. Include practical examples of creating new stores, managing complex state objects, and implementing state synchronization across components. Address performance optimization techniques, state debugging, and best practices for state management in desktop applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend-Backend Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for RDMM's frontend-backend integration using Tauri framework. Explain the command system architecture, IPC communication patterns, and security model implementation. Document the Rust backend services, Tauri command handlers, and data serialization mechanisms. Detail the integration points between React frontend and Rust backend, including type safety, error handling, and async operation management. Include practical examples of command invocation, data exchange patterns, and resource management. Address security considerations, sandboxing, and capability-based permissions. Provide guidelines for extending the command system, adding new backend services, and optimizing performance through efficient data transfer.

wiki_catalogs.name: LAN Chat
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's LAN chat plugin focused on peer-to-peer communication and local network collaboration. Explain the implementation of local network discovery, peer-to-peer messaging protocols, and real-time chat window management. Document the message preview system, file transfer capabilities, and room-based communication features. Detail the LAN discovery mechanisms, network topology handling, and security considerations for local network communication. Include practical examples of setting up LAN chat, discovering nearby peers, sending messages and files, and managing chat rooms. Address network configuration requirements, firewall considerations, and troubleshooting local network connectivity issues for seamless peer-to-peer communication.

wiki_catalogs.name: Developer Console
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Developer Console focused on system monitoring, debugging tools, and development utilities. Explain the console interface for accessing internal application logs, debugging information, and system status reports. Document the available debugging commands, log filtering capabilities, and diagnostic tools. Detail the integration with application internals, performance monitoring features, and troubleshooting workflows. Include practical examples of common debugging scenarios, log analysis techniques, and system health monitoring. Address security considerations, access controls, and best practices for development and debugging workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Encryption and Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's encryption and security implementation focused on data protection and secure communication. Explain the AES-256-GCM encryption algorithm usage, key derivation, and secure storage mechanisms. Document credential encryption, secure memory management, and cryptographic best practices. Detail the implementation of secure communication channels, certificate handling, and authentication flows. Include practical examples of encrypting sensitive data, implementing secure key storage, and handling cryptographic operations safely. Address security auditing, vulnerability assessment, and compliance considerations. Document the integration with operating system security features and platform-specific security implementations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Document Browser
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the MongoDB document browser focused on viewing, editing, and managing individual documents within collections. Document the document grid interface for displaying collection documents with pagination, sorting, and filtering capabilities. Explain the JSON editor interface for modifying document fields, inline editing features, and validation feedback. Detail the document operations including adding new documents, editing existing documents, deleting documents, and bulk operations. Include the document metadata display showing creation dates, modification history, and document size information. Address the integration with MongoDB's ObjectId handling, BSON type support, and proper JSON serialization/deserialization. Provide practical examples of document manipulation workflows, batch operations, and data validation scenarios. Explain error handling for invalid JSON, permission issues, and document size limitations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Table Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for MySQL table operations within RDMM. Document the table data viewer interface for browsing, searching, and editing table records with pagination, sorting, and filtering capabilities. Explain the import/export functionality for data migration including CSV import/export, bulk operations, and format conversion. Detail the record editing features with inline editing, batch operations, and data validation. Cover table structure management including column modifications, constraint updates, and schema evolution. Include practical examples of browsing large datasets, performing bulk data operations, exporting query results, importing CSV files, and managing table relationships. Address performance considerations for large table operations, transaction handling, backup strategies, and data integrity verification during imports/exports.

wiki_catalogs.name: Object Browser
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the S3 object browsing and management system. Document the object list interface for navigating file hierarchies within buckets, object metadata display, and file preview capabilities. Explain object operations including upload, download, delete, and rename functions. Detail the metadata drawer for viewing and editing object properties, tags, and custom metadata. Document the preview modal for supported file types including images, text files, and documents. Include practical examples of browsing object hierarchies, previewing files, managing object metadata, generating presigned URLs for temporary access, and handling bulk operations. Address performance considerations for large object lists, streaming downloads, and thumbnail generation for image previews.

wiki_catalogs.name: Redis Console
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Redis console interface focused on interactive command execution and real-time monitoring. Document the console command input system, command history and autocomplete functionality, response formatting and syntax highlighting, and batch command execution capabilities. Explain Redis command reference integration, parameter validation, error handling, and response parsing for different command outputs. Include practical examples of executing common Redis commands (GET, SET, HGETALL, LRANGE, SMEMBERS, ZRANGE), pipeline operations, transaction handling, and monitoring commands (INFO, CLIENT LIST, CONFIG GET). Address console customization options, command templates, script execution, and integration with the active connection context.

wiki_catalogs.name: SSH Key Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for SSH key management functionality. Document key import and export operations, key storage and organization, and key validation processes. Explain supported key formats, key generation, and key pair management. Detail key security practices, passphrase protection, and key rotation procedures. Cover key import from various sources, key metadata management, and key usage tracking. Include practical examples of importing SSH keys, generating new key pairs, and managing key collections. Address key security best practices, common key format issues, and troubleshooting key-related authentication problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environments View
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Environments View component focused on variable management and configuration. Document environment creation for different stages (development, staging, production), variable definition and substitution patterns, and secure credential storage. Explain environment switching mechanisms, variable inheritance, and override strategies. Detail template variables, dynamic value generation, and environment-specific configuration. Include practical examples of multi-environment workflows, sensitive data handling, and team collaboration patterns. Address environment validation, secret management, and compliance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Message Studio
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the MQ client message studio for crafting and sending custom messages. Include practical examples of creating test messages, setting up message headers, configuring routing properties, and testing message delivery. wiki_catalogs.name: Page Tree Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Confluence page tree navigation system. Document the hierarchical page structure visualization, navigation controls, and page management functionality. Document the sidebar interaction patterns, drag-and-drop functionality, and page creation/deletion workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Edge Overlay Resize System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the edge overlay resize system focused on custom window resizing functionality for non-native title bar environments. wiki_catalogs.name: Core Application Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's core application components focused on the main application shell and foundational systems. Detail the window management capabilities, responsive design patterns, and cross-platform desktop integration features. Include practical examples of customizing the UI, managing application state, and extending the layout system. Address accessibility considerations, performance optimization, and integration with the plugin architecture.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the S3 client plugin focused on Amazon S3-compatible storage management within RDMM. Explain the integration with RDMM's plugin architecture and file system operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for RDMM's security architecture and encryption implementation. Explain the AES-256-GCM encryption system, credential storage mechanisms, and secure communication protocols. Document the security model for database connections, SSH keys, and sensitive data handling. Detail the encryption key management, secure memory handling, and protection against common vulnerabilities. Include practical examples of secure credential storage, encrypted connection strings, and secure plugin communication. Address threat modeling, security best practices, and compliance considerations. wiki_catalogs.name: Plugin Implementations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's plugin implementation system focused on extending backend functionality through modular components. Explain the plugin architecture, command registration, and state management patterns. Document the implementation of each core plugin including API debugger, Confluence editor, LAN chat, database clients, and network tools. Detail the plugin lifecycle, initialization procedures, and cleanup processes. Include practical examples of implementing custom plugins, handling plugin dependencies, and managing plugin state. Address plugin isolation, resource management, and performance considerations. Document the relationship between plugins and the main application, including inter-plugin communication patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Query Workspace
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the MongoDB query workspace focused on executing MongoDB queries and aggregation pipelines within RDMM. Document the query editor interface supporting MongoDB query syntax, aggregation pipeline construction, and syntax highlighting. Explain the query execution environment including parameter binding, variable substitution, and result formatting options. Detail the supported query types including find operations, aggregation pipelines, map-reduce operations, and administrative commands. Include the result visualization options covering table display, JSON viewer, and chart generation for query results. Address query performance monitoring, execution time tracking, and result pagination for large datasets. Provide practical examples of common query patterns, complex aggregation pipelines, and performance optimization techniques. Document the integration with MongoDB's query planner, explain plans, and debugging tools available within the workspace.

wiki_catalogs.name: SQL Workspace
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the MySQL SQL workspace interface. Document the SQL editor component with syntax highlighting, auto-completion, query formatting, and execution history. Explain the query execution environment including result set visualization, performance analysis, and query plan inspection. Detail the workspace tabs for organizing multiple SQL sessions, saved queries, and query templates. Cover advanced SQL features including prepared statements, stored procedure execution, transaction management, and error handling. Include practical examples of writing complex queries, analyzing query performance, managing database transactions, and using SQL templates for repetitive operations. Address SQL development best practices, debugging techniques, result export options, and integration with MySQL client's connection management system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Workspace Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the S3 client workspace navigation system. Document the segmented tab interface for switching between connections, buckets, and objects views. Explain workspace state management including active connection tracking, selected bucket persistence, and navigation history. Detail the workspace tab switching logic, automatic bucket listing when entering buckets view, and active connection indicators. Include practical examples of navigating between different workspace views, maintaining context during navigation, and handling workspace state restoration. Address workspace performance optimization, lazy loading of workspace components, and memory management for large workspace states. Document the integration with RDMM's plugin routing system and workspace persistence mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Server Information and Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Redis server information and monitoring system. Detail the server info panel displaying real-time metrics including uptime, memory usage, connected clients, network statistics, and key space information. Document performance monitoring capabilities showing CPU utilization, memory fragmentation, hit ratios, and command processing rates. Explain server configuration display, database statistics, replication status, and persistence information. Include practical examples of interpreting server metrics, identifying performance bottlenecks, monitoring memory usage trends, and troubleshooting common Redis issues. Address automatic refresh mechanisms, historical data tracking, alert thresholds, and integration with external monitoring systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: SSH Tunnel Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for SSH tunnel management functionality. Detail tunnel rule creation, configuration, and management. Explain local and remote port forwarding, dynamic tunneling, and tunnel lifecycle management. Document tunnel validation, conflict detection, and tunnel status monitoring. Cover tunnel security considerations, access control, and tunnel cleanup procedures. Include practical examples of setting up port forwarding tunnels, configuring tunnel rules, and managing active tunnels. Address common tunnel configuration issues, port conflicts, and troubleshooting tunnel connectivity problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: History View
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the History View component focused on tracking and analyzing past API requests. Document request logging, search and filtering capabilities, and timeline visualization. Explain history organization, bookmarking features, and quick access shortcuts. Detail performance metrics tracking, error analysis, and trend identification. Include practical examples of debugging workflows, regression testing, and performance monitoring. Address history cleanup policies, export capabilities, and integration with external analytics tools. Cover privacy considerations and data retention strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: History Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the MQ client history tracking system. Explain the logging and audit trail functionality for message operations including sent messages, received messages, and connection activities. Document the history storage mechanism, retention policies, and search capabilities. Detail the interface for reviewing operation history, filtering by date ranges, and exporting historical data. Include examples of tracking message delivery confirmations, monitoring connection status changes, and analyzing message flow patterns over time. Address performance considerations for large history datasets, data cleanup procedures, and integration with external logging systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Attachment Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Confluence attachment management functionality. Detail the file upload processes, attachment handling workflows, and media integration capabilities. Explain the supported file formats, size limitations, and storage integration with Confluence. Document the attachment preview functionality, metadata extraction, and file organization within document contexts. Include practical examples of image embedding, document linking, and media management workflows. Address attachment security, access control, and versioning within the attachment system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Client Plugins
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's database client plugins focused on the integrated database management tools. Explain the unified approach to managing multiple database types through a consistent plugin interface. Document the connection management systems, query workspaces, and browser interfaces for MongoDB, MySQL, Redis, and S3. Detail the authentication mechanisms, connection pooling, and security implementations. Include practical examples of connecting to databases, executing queries, managing schemas, and performing administrative tasks. Address performance considerations, error handling, and best practices for each database type. Explain the shared patterns across plugins and unique features of each database client.

wiki_catalogs.name: Server Status
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the MongoDB server status monitoring interface focused on real-time server health and performance metrics within RDMM. Document the server information dashboard displaying database version, build information, and server configuration details. Explain the performance metrics visualization including connection statistics, memory usage, disk I/O operations, and query performance indicators. Detail the operational metrics such as command rates, cache hit ratios, replication lag, and database sizing information. Include the diagnostic tools for checking server health, identifying performance bottlenecks, and monitoring cluster status for replica sets and sharded clusters. Address the real-time data updates, metric refresh intervals, and historical trend analysis capabilities. Provide practical examples of interpreting server metrics, troubleshooting performance issues, and optimizing MongoDB deployments based on monitoring data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Server Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for MySQL server monitoring capabilities within RDMM. Document the server status dashboard displaying real-time metrics including connection counts, query performance, memory usage, disk I/O, and thread activity. Explain the performance indicators such as slow query detection, lock monitoring, buffer pool statistics, and replication status. Detail the server configuration insights including variable tuning recommendations, optimization suggestions, and capacity planning metrics. Cover alert systems for detecting performance degradation, connection spikes, and resource exhaustion. Include practical examples of interpreting server metrics, identifying performance bottlenecks, monitoring replication health, and implementing proactive maintenance strategies. Address integration with MySQL's performance schema, monitoring best practices, and troubleshooting common server performance issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Network Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's network tools focused on connectivity and communication utilities. Explain the integrated SSH client for secure terminal access, MQ client for message queue management (Kafka, RabbitMQ), and LAN chat for peer-to-peer communication. Document the connection management, authentication methods, and protocol implementations. Detail the terminal emulation capabilities, message browsing interfaces, and real-time messaging features. Include practical examples of setting up SSH tunnels, monitoring message queues, and collaborating through LAN chat. Address security considerations, performance optimization, and troubleshooting network connectivity issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Import & Export
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for MongoDB import and export functionality focused on data migration and backup operations within RDMM. Document the import interface supporting various file formats including JSON, CSV, and BSON for bulk data ingestion into MongoDB collections. Explain the export functionality for extracting collection data in multiple formats, selective field export, and filtered data export based on query conditions. Detail the import/export configuration options including batch processing, error handling, progress tracking, and resume capabilities for large datasets. Include the data transformation features for format conversion, field mapping, and data validation during import/export operations. Address performance optimization techniques for bulk operations, memory management, and concurrent processing. Provide practical examples of common import/export scenarios, data migration workflows, and backup strategies. Document error handling for malformed data, size limitations, and network interruptions during large data transfers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Utilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's development utility plugins focused on productivity and collaboration tools. Explain the API debugger for testing and debugging HTTP requests, the Confluence editor for collaborative documentation, and the developer console for system monitoring and debugging. Document the request/response handling, collection management, and environment configuration for the API debugger. Detail the markdown editing capabilities, real-time preview, publishing workflow, and attachment management for the Confluence editor. Include practical examples of API testing workflows, collaborative document creation, and system debugging techniques. Address integration patterns and best practices for each utility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Index Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for MongoDB index management focused on creating, monitoring, and optimizing database indexes within RDMM. Document the index browser interface for viewing existing indexes, their properties, and performance characteristics. Explain the index creation wizard supporting various index types including single field, compound, text, geospatial, and hashed indexes. Detail the index analysis tools for evaluating index usage, identifying unused indexes, and recommending optimization opportunities. Include the index maintenance operations such as rebuilding indexes, dropping unnecessary indexes, and monitoring index growth. Address the impact assessment tools for understanding the trade-offs between read performance improvements and write overhead. Provide practical examples of index optimization strategies, performance tuning workflows, and troubleshooting index-related performance issues. Document best practices for index design, monitoring index effectiveness, and managing index lifecycle in production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's backend integration focused on the Tauri/Rust system architecture. Explain the Tauri command system for frontend-backend communication, the database repository pattern for data access, and the encryption and security implementations. Document the plugin command system, async operation handling, and resource management. Detail the SQLite integration, credential storage, and cross-platform desktop capabilities. Include practical examples of implementing custom commands, managing database connections, and handling security-sensitive operations. Address performance considerations, error handling strategies, and debugging techniques for the backend system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing and Quality Assurance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's testing strategy and quality assurance processes focused on the automated testing framework and CI/CD pipeline. Explain the Vitest-based unit testing approach, component testing strategies, and integration testing patterns. Document the continuous integration workflows, build automation, and release management processes. Detail the testing best practices, mock strategies, and quality metrics. Include practical examples of writing tests for plugins, components, and backend services. Address performance testing, security auditing, and code coverage analysis. Explain the testing infrastructure and how to contribute to the testing ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build and Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's build configuration and deployment processes focused on the development workflow and distribution pipeline. Explain the Vite build system configuration, TypeScript setup, and asset management. Document the Tauri build process, platform-specific compilation, and packaging procedures. Detail the CI/CD workflows, automated testing, and release management. Include practical examples of local development setup, building for different platforms, and deploying releases. Address optimization strategies, bundle size management, and troubleshooting build issues. Explain the deployment topology and distribution channels.

wiki_catalogs.name: Advanced Topics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for RDMM's advanced topics focused on extensibility, performance optimization, and expert-level usage. Explain the plugin development framework, custom plugin creation, and extension patterns. Document performance optimization techniques including memory management, connection pooling, and lazy loading strategies. Detail the open specification system, agent integrations, and Claude AI capabilities. Include practical examples of developing custom plugins, optimizing application performance, and integrating with external systems. Address advanced configuration options, troubleshooting complex issues, and contributing to the open source ecosystem. Explain the extensibility points and how to leverage them effectively.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting content for RDMM focused on common issues, debugging techniques, and frequently asked questions. Document systematic approaches to diagnosing problems, using the developer console for log analysis, and interpreting error messages. Include solutions for installation problems, plugin loading errors, connection issues, and performance problems. Detail the debugging tools available, log analysis techniques, and error reporting procedures. Provide step-by-step resolution guides for common scenarios and escalation paths for complex issues. Address platform-specific problems, compatibility issues, and migration challenges. Include preventive measures and best practices to avoid common pitfalls.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# RDMM: Developer Desktop Toolbox - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
RDMM (Developer Nexus) is a comprehensive desktop application designed to serve as a unified development toolkit, providing developers with integrated access to multiple database clients, network tools, and development utilities through a single, cohesive interface.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Unified Development Environment**: Consolidate various developer tools into a single desktop application
- **Cross-Platform Compatibility**: Leverage Tauri framework for native desktop experiences across Windows, macOS, and Linux
- **Plugin Architecture**: Enable extensible functionality through a modular plugin system
- **Developer Productivity**: Streamline common development tasks like database management, SSH access, and API testing

### Target Audience
- Software developers and engineers requiring multiple development tools
- DevOps professionals managing infrastructure and databases
- Teams seeking centralized development utilities
- Developers working in diverse technology stacks requiring cross-platform solutions

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Main Application] --> B[Plugin Registry System]
    A --> C[UI Shell & Layout]
    A --> D[State Management]
    
    B --> E[Database Clients]
    B --> F[Network Tools]
    B --> G[Development Utilities]
    
    E --> H[MongoDB Client]
    E --> I[MySQL Client]
    E --> J[Redis Manager]
    E --> K[S3 Client]
    
    F --> L[SSH Client]
    F --> M[MQ Client]
    F --> N[Network Diagnostics]
    
    G --> O[API Debugger]
    G --> P[Confluence Editor]
    G --> Q[Lan Chat]
    
    C --> R[AppShell Layout]
    C --> S[Sidebar Navigation]
    C --> T[Status Bar]
    
    D --> U[Zustand Store]
    D --> V[Theme Management]
    D --> W[Settings Store]
```

### Design Patterns

**Plugin Architecture Pattern**: The application uses a registry-based plugin system allowing dynamic loading and management of different tool modules.

**Repository Pattern**: Each plugin maintains its own data access layer through dedicated repositories for database connections and operations.

**Observer Pattern**: Real-time updates are implemented through reactive state management and periodic polling mechanisms.

**Factory Pattern**: Plugin registration and instantiation follows factory-style patterns for consistent initialization.

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant AppShell
    participant PluginRouter
    participant PluginRegistry
    participant PluginModule
    
    User->>AppShell: Select Tool
    AppShell->>PluginRouter: Route to Plugin
    PluginRouter->>PluginRegistry: Get Plugin Manifest
    PluginRegistry->>PluginModule: Initialize Plugin
    PluginModule-->>PluginRouter: Render Interface
    PluginRouter-->>AppShell: Display Plugin View
    AppShell-->>User: Show Tool Interface
```

### Data Flow Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Input] --> B[Plugin Router]
    B --> C[Plugin Registry]
    C --> D[Plugin Handler]
    D --> E[Data Repository]
    E --> F[External Service/API]
    F --> E
    E --> D
    D --> G[State Update]
    G --> H[UI Re-render]
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Bootstrap** (`src/main.tsx`)
- Initializes React application with Ant Design theming
- Registers built-in plugins during startup
- Sets up theme management and global styling
- Configures Tauri integration for desktop features

**Application Shell** (`src/app/layout/AppShell.tsx`)
- Main layout container with sidebar navigation
- Status bar integration with real-time system monitoring
- Plugin routing and content management
- Window resize capabilities for desktop experience

### Core Modules

**Plugin Registry System** (`src/app/plugin-registry/registry.ts`)
- Centralized plugin management and registration
- Sidebar ordering and visibility control
- Dynamic plugin discovery and loading
- Type-safe plugin manifest handling

**Plugin Router** (`src/app/plugin-registry/PluginRouter.tsx`)
- Route-based plugin rendering
- Active plugin state management
- Navigation between different development tools
- Integration with sidebar navigation

### Configuration Approach

**Frontend Dependencies** (`package.json`)
- React 19 with modern hooks and concurrent features
- Ant Design for UI components and theming
- Zustand for lightweight state management
- Monaco Editor for code editing capabilities

**Backend Dependencies** (`src-tauri/Cargo.toml`)
- Tauri 2.x for cross-platform desktop integration
- Rust-based plugins for enhanced security and performance
- Database drivers for MongoDB, MySQL, Redis, S3
- Network libraries for SSH, MQ, and HTTP operations

### External Dependencies

**Frontend Libraries**
- `@tauri-apps/api` - Desktop platform integration
- `@monaco-editor/react` - Code editing capabilities
- `antd` - UI component library with dark mode support
- `zustand` - State management solution

**Backend Services**
- `redis`, `mongodb`, `mysql_async` - Database connectivity
- `aws-sdk-s3` - Cloud storage integration
- `russh` - Secure shell protocol implementation
- `reqwest` - HTTP client with TLS support

### Integration Points

**Desktop Platform Integration** (`src-tauri/src/lib.rs`)
- File system access and dialog management
- Native window controls and drag regions
- Clipboard and system notifications
- Process spawning and inter-process communication

**Plugin Communication** (`src/app/plugin-registry/types.ts`)
- Standardized plugin interface contracts
- Event-driven communication patterns
- Shared state management across plugins
- Type-safe plugin-to-plugin messaging

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class AppShell {
        +sidebarCollapsed: boolean
        +selectedPluginId: string
        +lanSnapshot: LanChatSnapshot
        +render(): JSX.Element
    }
    
    class PluginRegistry {
        +register(plugin: PluginManifest): void
        +getAll(): PluginManifest[]
        +getById(id: string): PluginManifest
    }
    
    class PluginRouter {
        +activePlugin: string
        +navigate(pluginId: string): void
        +renderActivePlugin(): JSX.Element
    }
    
    class ThemeStore {
        +mode: 'light' | 'dark'
        +toggleTheme(): void
        +setTheme(mode: string): void
    }
    
    AppShell --> PluginRouter
    AppShell --> ThemeStore
    PluginRouter --> PluginRegistry
    PluginRegistry --> PluginManifest
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multi-Database Management**
- MongoDB client with connection pooling and query workspace
- MySQL client with schema browser and SQL editor
- Redis manager with key-value browsing and TTL management
- S3 client for cloud storage operations

**Network and Infrastructure Tools**
- SSH client with terminal emulation and tunnel management
- MQ client supporting Kafka and RabbitMQ protocols
- LAN chat for peer-to-peer communication
- Network diagnostics and connection testing

**Development Utilities**
- API debugger with request/response logging
- Confluence editor for documentation collaboration
- Developer console with debugging capabilities
- Environment variable management

### Implementation Highlights

**Real-Time Monitoring** (`src/app/layout/status-bar.ts`)
- Live system status indicators
- LAN chat activity tracking
- Database connection health monitoring
- Performance metrics display

**Plugin Architecture** (`src/app/plugin-registry/builtin.ts`)
- Built-in plugin registration system
- Automatic plugin discovery and loading
- Sidebar integration for all available tools
- Consistent UI patterns across plugins

**Cross-Platform Support** (`src/app/runtime/platform.ts`)
- Runtime detection for desktop vs browser
- Platform-specific UI adaptations
- Native window controls and behaviors
- File system integration patterns

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Loading
    Loading --> PluginRegistration: Initialize Plugins
    PluginRegistration --> AppShell: Setup Complete
    AppShell --> SidebarNavigation: Ready
    SidebarNavigation --> PluginSelection: User Click
    PluginSelection --> PluginExecution: Load Plugin
    PluginExecution --> DataOperation: User Action
    DataOperation --> ResultDisplay: Operation Complete
    ResultDisplay --> PluginSelection: Continue Working
    PluginSelection --> SidebarNavigation: Switch Tool
    SidebarNavigation --> AppShell: Navigation Complete
    AppShell --> [*]: Application Exit
```

**Performance Considerations**
- Lazy loading of plugin components to minimize startup time
- Connection pooling for database and network operations
- Debounced real-time updates to prevent excessive polling
- Efficient state management with selective re-renders

**Security Implementation**
- Encrypted credential storage using AES-GCM encryption
- Secure SSH key management and validation
- HTTPS/TLS enforcement for all external communications
- Sandboxed plugin execution environments

**Extensibility Framework**
- Plugin manifest system for third-party extensions
- Standardized API for plugin-to-plugin communication
- Configuration-driven plugin customization
- Hot-swappable plugin architecture

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [Cargo.toml](src-tauri/Cargo.toml)
- [App.tsx](src/App.tsx)
- [main.tsx](src/main.tsx)
- [AppShell.tsx](src/app/layout/AppShell.tsx)
- [registry.ts](src/app/plugin-registry/registry.ts)

</blog>
